## Script #1 — 90-Day Joint & Recovery Timeline (Actor UGC)

**Persona:** Fitness / Joint & Recovery
**Structure:** Progression timeline (UGC Green Screen)
**Duration:** ~50 seconds
**Caption style:** Bold sans-serif + milestone cards (body) / Subtitle-only auto-generated style (hooks)

### Concept

A fit young male actor speaks directly to camera about his 90-day recovery transformation on IM8. Same milestone framework as T001 Script #1 (first sip, Day 9, Day 21, Day 90), same key ingredients, but entirely rewritten copy with fresh scaffolding, failed alternatives woven into the body, and distinct hook/close executions. The UGC green-screen format over gym B-roll feels native and unproduced. The actor's own training experience anchors every claim.

### Top Spender Translation

| Element                         | Source                  | Adaptation                                                             |
| ------------------------------- | ----------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Temporal discounting milestones | Ad 02 ($111K, 69 hook)  | Four escalating milestones, each naming ingredient + mechanism.        |
| UGC Green Screen                | Ad 04 ($90K, 63 hook)   | Actor face-to-camera in corner/half-screen composited over gym B-roll. |
| Simple direct language          | Ad 05 ($84K, 0.90 ROAS) | Short declarative sentences, pain-first, easy to follow.               |
| Proof barrage close             | Ad 02                   | NSF + Beckham + clinical trial stat. VO emotional, caption proof.      |

## Script #2 — Train Harder, Recover Faster

**Persona:** Fitness / Athlete
**Structure:** Mechanism explainer
**Duration:** ~46 seconds
**Caption style:** Two-tier: sans-serif narration + bold SERIF keywords

### Concept

Targets the fitness athlete whose training intensity exceeds their recovery capacity. Opens with the felt gap between effort in the gym and results outside it, then delivers a three-ingredient mechanism chain: amino complex for muscle repair, CoQ10 for cellular energy recovery, and anti-inflammatory compounds for clearing post-workout inflammation. The Plateful editing style drives urgency with 1.3-2.0s shots, hard cuts, color-coded states, and hooks that lead with motion or CGI rather than face-to-camera. Temporal discounting language anchors immediate benefits.

### Top Spender Translation

| Element | Source | Adaptation |
|---|---|---|
| Shot pacing 1.3-2.0s, hard cuts | Plateful Ref A (59s) | Same relentless rhythm across hooks and body. No fancy transitions. |
| Numbered mechanism framework | Plateful Ref A ("better for three reasons") | Three-step recovery mechanism: muscle repair, cellular energy, inflammation |
| Color-coded states (B&W pain, saturated solution) | Plateful Ref C, Ref D | Desaturated/B&W for soreness beats, full color at recovery payoff |
| Split-screen cause/effect | Plateful Ref A (split: good pan vs bad pan) | Split-screen: athlete grinding + CGI muscle fiber repair underneath |
| CGI body process visualization | Ad 03 (gut timeline, ROAS 1.18) | CGI muscle fiber tearing and rebuilding, mitochondria producing ATP |
| Past-perfect milestone framing | Ad 03 ("have cleared," "have colonized") | "Your amino acids have already started rebuilding muscle tissue" |

---

- **B-roll:** Actor training hard (deadlifts, box jumps, sprints, battle ropes), recovery moments (stretching, foam rolling, drinking IM8 post-workout). Mix UGC-feel gym footage with polished product shots.
- **CGI:** Two cutaways maximum. (1) Muscle fiber cross-section showing micro-tears repairing. (2) Mitochondria producing ATP energy particles. Both under 4s each, intercut with lifestyle B-roll.
- **Edit pace:** 1.3-2.0s shots throughout per Plateful reference. Hard cuts only. No transitions.

## Script #3 — Recovery Reframe — Not Age

**Persona:** Fitness / Athlete
**Structure:** Mechanism explainer
**Duration:** ~48 seconds
**Caption style:** Two-tier: sans-serif narration + bold SERIF keywords

### Concept

Challenges the "I'm just getting older" belief that active adults use to explain declining recovery. Opens with a visceral pain moment before the contrarian flip (per concept validation flag), then reveals that nutrient gaps, not aging, are behind slower recovery. The top spenders editing style keeps it casual and credible: face-to-camera trust path, longer takes, no music, CGI as a single pattern interrupt for nutrient absorption visualization. Educational angle that reframes the problem without attacking the viewer's identity as an athlete.

### Top Spender Translation

| Element | Source | Adaptation |
|---|---|---|
| Creator trust path, casual pacing | Ad 04 ($79K, watch 66) | Actor speaking to camera, no music, longer takes, personal credibility |
| "What happens when" curiosity framing | Ad 03 (ROAS 1.18) | "Here's what actually happens to your recovery when you fill the gaps" |
| CGI body process (single-focus) | Ad 03 | One CGI sequence: nutrient absorption pathway from gut to muscle tissue |
| Past-perfect milestone language | Ad 03 ("have cleared," "have colonized") | "Your muscles have started receiving the nutrients they've been missing" |
| Social proof stacking in close | Ad 02 ($108K, hook 70) | Reviews, clinical trial, NSF stacked at end |

---

- **CGI:** One cutaway maximum. Nutrient absorption: cross-section of intestinal villi absorbing nutrient particles that travel through bloodstream to muscle tissue. Under 5s.

## Script #4 — Gut Health 90-Day Timeline

**Persona:** Gut Health
**Structure:** Progression timeline (CGI)
**Duration:** ~50 seconds
**Caption style:** Bold sans-serif + milestone cards

### Concept

A CGI-driven 90-day gut health timeline replicating Ad 3's single-focus approach (ROAS 1.18). Each milestone maps to one tier of the 4-tier gut system: enzymes clear the path, prebiotics prepare the environment, probiotics colonize, postbiotics seal the lining. Split-screen format pairs a person sipping IM8 with CGI gut interior visualizations. Past-perfect milestone framing ("have broken down," "have colonized") treats each benefit as an accomplished fact. Upbeat 120-125 BPM instrumental drives the pace.

### Top Spender Translation

| Element | Source | Adaptation |
|---|---|---|
| Split-screen sipping + CGI gut | Ad 3 ($82K, ROAS 1.18) | Same juxtaposition of person drinking and CGI interior, adapted for 4-tier gut system milestones |
| Past-perfect milestone framing | Ad 3 | "Have broken down," "have colonized," "have sealed" — same accomplished-fact phrasing |
| "What happens when" curiosity hook | Ad 3 + Ad 5 ($78K) | Same hook formula adapted for gut-specific 90-day window |
| Social proof stacking close | Ad 3 + Ad 2 ($108K) | 85% digestion stat + NSF + Beckham — same layered trust format |

- **B-roll:** Person sipping IM8 in morning routine, meal prep with whole foods, comfortable evening relaxation. Grounded lifestyle moments.

## Script #14 — Multi-System Reset

**Persona:** Menopause
**Structure:** Numbered reasons
**Duration:** ~49 seconds
**Caption style:** Bold sans-serif + milestone cards

### Concept

A numbered-reasons format that breaks menopause into 7 body systems and shows how 90 ingredients address each one. Each system gets a single beat with its own mini-PAS: name the system failing, name the ingredient solving it, and move on. The Plateful editing style drives the pace: 1.3-2.0s hard cuts, hooks that start with CGI/motion not face-to-camera, color-coded states (B&W for pain, saturated for solution), and keyword-emphasized captions. Temporal discounting is woven in through explicit early-benefit language.

### Top Spender Translation

| Element | Source | Adaptation |
|---|---|---|
| Numbered framework | Plateful Ref A ("better for three reasons") | "7 systems" framework, each reason = one system with one ingredient |
| B&W pain / saturated solution color coding | Plateful Ref C (B&W for competitor pans, color for Plateful) | B&W for each failing system, saturated when ingredient is named |
| Hook starts with motion/CGI, not face-to-camera | Plateful pattern (all 4 refs) | CGI body systems overview as hook visual |
| 1.3-2.0s hard cuts | Plateful Ref C (1.3s avg), Ref D (1.3s avg) | Matching cut rate across all body beats |
| Split screens for cause/effect | Plateful Ref A (smooth vs textured pan) | Split: B&W symptom footage / color solution footage per system |

## Script #15 — Coffee + IM8 (Lasting Energy)

**Persona:** Energy Seeker
**Structure:** Mechanism explainer
**Duration:** ~48 seconds
**Caption style:** Two-tier: sans-serif narration + bold SERIF keywords

### Concept

Validates the viewer's coffee habit instead of attacking it, then introduces IM8 as the missing second layer for lasting energy. Coffee handles the morning spike. IM8 handles what happens after. The mechanism chain: CoQ10 at 100mg supports mitochondrial ATP production, active B vitamins convert food into energy the body can use, and amino acids provide the raw material for sustained cellular function. Permission-first framing removes the guilt of caffeine use and positions IM8 as a complement, not a replacement. Temporal discounting language anchors early benefits.

## Script #16 — Afternoon Crash 90-Day Timeline

**Persona:** Energy Seeker
**Structure:** Progression timeline (CGI)
**Duration:** ~49 seconds
**Caption style:** Bold sans-serif + milestone cards

### Concept

A 90-day energy timeline for the caffeine-dependent, afternoon-crashing Energy Seeker. The mechanism focuses on CoQ10 for mitochondrial ATP production, active B vitamins for sustained energy metabolism, and the amino complex for cellular fuel. Each milestone is an accomplished fact in past-perfect tense. The hook taps the universal 2pm wall experience. Single-focus on energy throughout, never drifting into gut or sleep or joints.

### Top Spender Translation

| Element | Source | Adaptation |
|---|---|---|
| "What happens when" curiosity hook | Ad 03 ($81K, ROAS 1.18) | Same curiosity structure, reframed for energy: "what happens to your energy" |
| Past-perfect milestone framing | Ad 03 | "Has started producing," "have been converting," "has become" |
| Single-focus CGI body process | Ad 03 (gut-only = best ROAS) | Energy-only focus: mitochondria, ATP production, cellular fuel |
| Split-screen (person + CGI) | Ad 03 (fit man sipping + CGI gut) | Person at desk/in daily life + CGI cellular energy underneath |
| Proof stacking close | Ad 03, Ad 05 | 95% energy stat + NSF + Beckham |

---

Reach Digital_IM8_Afternoon Crash Timeline_16_T003

### Visual Direction
- **B-roll:** Office/daily life scenarios (desk, commute, family evening) mixed with active lifestyle (walking, exercising at 6pm). Show the transformation from dragging through afternoons to having energy left at dinner.
- **CGI:** Mitochondrial energy production visualization (ATP molecules forming, electron transport chain, cellular power). Single-focus on energy mechanisms. 2-3s per cutaway, max 2 in the body.
- **Edit pace:** 1.5-2s cuts in hooks and milestones. Milestone card holds for 2s. 120-125 BPM upbeat instrumental.
- **Color:** Washed-out/desaturated for the crash state (hooks, Day 1), building to warm, vibrant, energized palette by Day 90 and close.

### References
**Top Spender**
- [Ad 03 - 90-Day Gut Health Timeline](https://projects.motionapp.com/organization/6214fe5e1ccc60d23897d87b/69c47a6384753eef7353f954/creative/69e5c774e23fb809273f3347)
	- Milestone card pacing (2s per card), single-focus CGI progression, split-screen hook format
- [Ad 05 - 30-Day Benefits Timeline (Vlad)](https://projects.motionapp.com/organization/6214fe5e1ccc60d23897d87b/69c47a6384753eef7353f954/creative/69c47b0d3ea1439320c76bad)
	- CGI vitamin orbs, intercut CGI + lifestyle B-roll rhythm

# End Cards

**Brand style direction (all end cards):** Deep crimson/red gradient background with slow-moving gradient light effect (modern gradient, not silk — some cards can use subtle silk for variety). Elegant serif font for headlines in semi-transparent dark maroon boxes. Text builds sequentially under 2s. Motion is cinemagraph or slow Ken Burns, not fast-cut. Pulsating CTA button (brand red, full-width) on every card.

**Music direction (all end cards):** Upbeat, energetic background music with gym energy — not silence. Think clean electronic or motivational beats that match the brand's premium fitness positioning. Music should feel aspirational and active, not ambient or meditative.

**Plateful format takeaways (structure only, not style):** B-roll video window as hero element, value props in pill format below, sequential element build. See [Plateful End Card refs](https://drive.google.com/drive/u/0/folders/1sfsn_tWuUXnCNHEEwuMDfVl3rohT-JgO) for format examples.

**Important:** This is face-to-camera only. No gym setups, no bed/morning setups — just face-to-camera against a clean background. We'll AI face/voice swap to male in post. All gym B-roll, morning stiffness B-roll, and lifestyle shots will use stock footage.
